images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of LMEB memory categories and specificities. Table1presents detailed dataset statistics. Tables5,6,7, and8provide examples of query-relevant document pairs for each dataset. Tables10,11,12, and13provide detailed task types and example abilities assessed.

(a)Inter-dataset diversity in LMEB. The left side illustrates pairwise weighted Jaccard Similarity (JS) scores between unigram word distributions of each dataset corpus, while the right side shows dataset relationships with a force-directed 2D layout.
(a)Performance comparison betweenw/o inst.andw/ inst.. The x-axis represents the two conditions (w/o inst.andw/ inst.), and the y-axis indicates the N@10 performance.

Figure 20:Correlation between the evaluation scores on LMEB and MTEB (eng, v2) (retrieval subset)Enevoldsen et al. (2025). The evaluation score for LMEB is based on the N@10 metric, aligned with MTEB (eng, v2) (retrieval subset). Mean (Dataset) scores underw/ inst.are used for LMEB. Note that bge-m3 (Dense), bge-large-en-v1.5, and EmbeddingGemma-300M perform better without task instructions, so their results are based on thew/o inst.setting. The size of the points in the plot is proportional to the size of the models.
Figure 21:Correlation between the scores on LMEB-Episodic and MTEB (eng, v2) (retrieval subset).
Figure 22:Correlation between the scores on LMEB-Dialogue and MTEB (eng, v2) (retrieval subset).
Figure 23:Correlation between the scores on LMEB-Semantic and MTEB (eng, v2) (retrieval subset).
Figure 24:Correlation between the scores on LMEB-Procedural and MTEB (eng, v2) (retrieval subset).
